content.full_text:
Re
Farn PolrucAt- PRACTTcES CoruurssroN
428 J Street o Suite 620 r Sacramento, CA 95814-2329
(916) 322-5660 . Fax (916) 322-0886
January 6,2004
Richard Cromwell,III
Post Office Box 1207
Desert Hot Springs, CA 92240
Your Request for Informal Assistance
Our FiIe No. l-03-294
Dear Mr. Cromwell:
This letter is in response to your request for clarification of advice given to you
under Cromwell Advice Letter, No. I-03-162,regarding the provisions of the Political
Reform Act (the "Act").I Since you are asking for advice o, th" general application of the
law, we are treating your request as one for informal assistance pursuant to regulation
18329(c).2 Nothing in this letter shall be construed to comment on any conduct that has
abeady occurred.
QUESTTONS
You have discovered that statements of economic interests you filed
previously contain errors. Are you required to file amendments to correct
the errors?
If the answer to question one is yes, how far back in history should the
amendments cover?
Is there a time limit for these amendments to be filed?
Where should you file the amendments?
If you are late in filing the amendments, what actions should you take to be
in compliance?
I Government Code sections 81000 - gl}l4. Commission regulations appear at Title 2, sections
18109-18997, of the California Code of Regulations.
2 Informal assistance does not provide the requestor with the immunity provided by an opinion or
formal written advice. (Government Code Section 83114;2 Cal. Code of Regs. section 18329(c)(3), copy
enclosed.)
1
2.
aJ.
4.
5.
iiiaiiF


File No. I-03-294
Page No. 2
CONCLUSIONS
Yes. In order to correct an error(s) contained on a statement of economic
interests that has been filed, an amendment to the statement correcting the
error(s) must be filed.
Amend the statements of economic interests that contain an error(s) that are
seven years old or less.
The Act does not specify a deadline by which the amendments must be
filed. However, we recoflmend that you file an amendment as soon as
practicable.
Amendments to statements of economic interests must be filed with the
same filing officer as the original statement(s) was filed.
As stated above, there is no deadline for filing amendments.
FACTS
In Cromwell Advice Letter, No. I-03-162,we advised thatit appeared you were
required to report the travel payments specified in your letter to us dated July 15, 2003, on
your statement of economic interests. The questions listed above indicate that you have
concluded the travel payments were reportable and that you need to amend a statement(s)
of economic interests previously filed.
ANALYSIS
1. 
In the Paul Advice Letter, No. I-03-218, we advised that a statement of economic
interests must be amended if information required to be reported under the Act was not
provided when the statement was originally filed. Since it appears you determined the
havel payrnents you received should have been reported on your statement of economic
interests, you must file an amendment to any statement previously filed that did not
contain the required information.
2. 
The Act does not specify a deadline by which an amended statement must be filed.
However, under section 81009(e), statements of economic interests filed by members of
the Sunline Transit Agency and Sunline Services Group Joint Powers Authorities must
be retained by the filing officer for not less than seven years. In addition, section 81002(c)
sets forth the purpose of requiring public officials to file statements of economic interests.
It states that:
"Assets and income of public officials which may be
materially affected by their official actions should be
disclosed and in appropriate circumstances the officials
should be disqualified from acting in order that conflicts of
interest may be avoided."
Since the purpose of an amendment is to correct erroneous information, or to provide
required information missing from the original filing, correcting an elror to a statement
that is no longer available to members of the public is unnecessary. Therefore, we
1
2
a
-)
4
5


File No. I-03-294
Page No. 3
recommendthat you amend through the applicable retention period as specified in section
81009. (Bloom Advice Letter, No. I-03-135.)
3. 
Section 81004.5 states that:
"Any report or statement file pursuant to this title may be
amended by the filer at any time. Amending an incorrect
or incomplete report or statementmay be considered as
evidence of good faith."
Thus, there is no specific deadline by which the amendment must be filed, although we
recoflrmend it be done as soon as practicable.
4. 
Section 87500(o) provides for where statements of economic interests must be
filed by persons who hold positions not otherwise listed in section 87500, such as
members of the board for Sunline Transit Agency and Sunline Services Group, both of
which are California Joint Powers Authorities. This section requires these officials to file:
"...one original with the agency or with the code reviewing
body, as provided by the code reviewing body in the
agency's conflict of interest code."
Amendments must be filed in the same location as the original statements they are
amending.
5. 
There is nothing in the Act that requires any actions, other than filing an
amendment under section 81004.5, when an error is discovered on a statement already
filed. As noted above, there is no specific deadline for filing amended statements.
If you have any other questions regarding this matter,please contact me at (916)
322-5660.
Sincerely,
Luisa Menchaca
General
By:
S
PhD
Political Reform Consultant II
Technical Assistance Division
Enclosure
KSMjg
